title: Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) Intuition
concept: BPE starts by tokenizing characters, then iteratively merges the most frequent adjacent byte pairs to build a vocabulary of common subwords.

exercises.prompt: Before diving into BPE, what is a 'token' in the context of AI?

options.text: A type of digital currency used to buy AI models.
The small chunks of text, like words or subwords, that an AI reads.
The physical cables connecting AI servers together.

exercises.explain: Tokens are the basic building blocks (like words or characters) that language models use to process and generate text.

cards.title: The Reading Dilemma
cards.body: How do computers read human books? If they read word-by-word, they get stuck on new words they've never seen before. If they read letter-by-letter, it takes forever! How do we find the perfect middle ground? Let's discover BPE!

cards.title: What is BPE?
cards.body: Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) is a smart way to break text into pieces called tokens. It starts with individual letters and repeatedly combines the most common side-by-side pairs (like 'd' and 'e' into 'de') until it builds a handy vocabulary of useful word parts.

cards.title: The LEGO Builder
cards.body: Think of building with LEGO bricks. Instead of starting with pre-made houses (whole words) or tiny individual plastic studs (single characters), you snap the most commonly paired studs together into small blocks (like 'th' or 'ing'). Soon, you have a set of custom, reusable blocks that make building any structure fast and easy!

cards.title: BPE in Action
cards.body: If your AI sees the words 'look', 'looking', and 'looks', it will notice that 'look' appears constantly. Instead of storing 'looking' and 'looks' as completely separate, giant words, BPE keeps 'look' as one piece, and adds 'ing' and 's' as separate pieces. This keeps the vocabulary neat and tidy!

cards.title: Myth: BPE Understands Grammar
cards.body: Many people think text tokenizers must know actual grammar rules to split words. In reality, BPE doesn't know what a verb or a noun is! It relies entirely on mathematical patterns and how often letters sit next to each other.

cards.title: Tokenizing 'singing'
cards.body: See how character-level tokenization compares to the smart subword chunks created by Byte Pair Encoding (BPE).

left.text: s - i - n - g - i - n - g (7 separate tokens, very slow for AI to read)

right.text: sing - ing (2 common, meaningful tokens, much faster to process)

exercises.prompt: How does BPE help an AI deal with rare or misspelled words?

options.text: It translates the text into multiple foreign languages.
It breaks unfamiliar words down into smaller, recognizable subword tokens.
It deletes rare words from the text completely to save space.
It replaces unknown words with random punctuation marks.

exercises.explain: BPE helps language models handle new or rare words by breaking them down into familiar subword parts, rather than seeing them as completely unknown.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Byte Pair Encoding starts at the character level and builds upward by merging common pairs.
exercises.explain: BPE starts with the smallest units (individual characters) and builds up to larger subwords and words by merging them.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of BPE's core process.
exercises.sentence: BPE works by repeatedly finding and merging the most common adjacent ___ of characters.
exercises.explain: BPE scans the text, finds which two characters appear next to each other most often, and merges them into a single token.

exercises.prompt:
Identify which prompt was written by the AI helper explaining BPE:
Prompt A: "BPE builds its vocabulary from the bottom up. It begins with individual characters and merges the most frequent adjacent pairs step-by-step."
Prompt B: "BPE works by analyzing the grammar of sentences and automatically splitting words into prefixes and suffixes using linguistic rules."
Prompt C: "BPE is an algorithm that randomly groups words together into pairs to compress database files for faster downloads."},practice_tf:{explain:
exercises.explain: Prompt A is the AI. BPE builds its vocabulary from the ground up by combining frequent characters, whereas WordPiece typically starts with characters and uses a likelihood formula (not just raw frequency counts) to merge them.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a brand-new AI. How would you explain to it the step-by-step process of Byte Pair Encoding?

options.text: It randomly groups letters together until the vocabulary is full.
It starts with single characters and repeatedly merges the most common adjacent pairs.
It splits whole dictionary words into equal halves.
It only keeps words that appear more than a hundred times in a dictionary.

exercises.explain: BPE starts with individual characters as tokens and iteratively merges the most frequent adjacent pairs. This process stops once the vocabulary reaches the target size you set.
